advantages: Hierarchical structure mirrors many real-world objects (documents, configs, plans).
Balanced BSTs give O(log n) for search, insert, delete, AND ordered iteration.
Range queries (`keys between a and b`) are natural — hash tables can't do this.
Recursive algorithms are short and expressive on trees.
disadvantages: Unbalanced BSTs degrade to O(n); maintaining balance requires extra machinery (AVL, red-black).
Python's stdlib doesn't include a balanced tree — you need sortedcontainers or similar.
Pointer-heavy: more memory per element than an array or hash table.
Deep recursion can blow Python's stack; use iterative traversals or `sys.setrecursionlimit` carefully.
